auditMetadata.updateReason: Reconciled with V2 engine migration completion (Dec 2025). Several PARTIAL features now IMPLEMENTED.
auditMetadata.analysisMethod: Comparison of documentation claims vs code implementation

features.name: Cloud Backup Sync
features.claim: Local-first Storage: SQLite-based local storage with optional cloud sync
features.analysis: Cloud sync mentioned in marketing but not implemented. Local-first blob storage is the architecture. This is vaporware - remove from docs or mark as 'planned'.

features.name: Timeline Integration
features.claim: Timeline Integration: View snapshots in VS Code's built-in Timeline
features.analysis: VERIFIED 2025-12-17: CheckpointTimelineProvider is fully implemented. registerTimelineProvider is called during activation. Integration tests exist.

features.name: File Health Indicators
features.claim: File Health Indicators: Visual indicators (🛡️/⚠️/🚨) show the health status of your files
features.analysis: Setting exists (snapback.showFileHealthDecorations). Decoration provider likely exists. Verify full implementation.

features.name: Full-text Search in Snapshots
features.claim: Full-text Search: Fast search through snapshot contents
features.analysis: Mentioned in README but no full-text search implementation found. This is vaporware.
dependencies: sqlite FTS5 or separate search index

features.name: Bulk Operations for Snapshots
features.claim: Bulk Operations: Delete older snapshots, rename snapshots, and protect/unprotect in bulk
features.analysis: deleteOlderSnapshots exists (line 140 per matrix). Multi-select bulk operations in tree view need verification.

features.name: Policy Override Creation
features.claim: Create Policy Override command available in context menu
features.analysis: VERIFIED 2025-12-17: Full implementation in policyOverrideCommands.ts. Registered via registerPolicyOverrideCommands. Has ProgressiveDisclosureController integration for ADVANCED users.

features.name: Open Snapshot in Web
features.claim: Open Snapshot in Web command to view snapshots in web console
features.analysis: VERIFIED 2025-12-17: Implemented in explorerTree.ts line 103. Has integration tests. Opens snapshot viewer in web console.

features.name: Session-Based Snapshots UI
features.claim: Session-Based Snapshots: Group related file changes into atomic sessions
features.analysis: Session commands (list, restore, export) are implemented per matrix. View defined in package.json. DBSCAN clustering provides intelligent grouping.

features.name: Phantom Dependency Detection
features.claim: Phantom Dependency Detection: Identifies missing dependencies that could break your application
features.analysis: FULLY IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine. Signal script extracts ES6/CommonJS/dynamic imports, ignores Node builtins and workspace packages, detects typosquatting.

features.name: AI Change Tracking
features.claim: AI Change Tracking: Detects AI-generated code and tracks changes for security auditing
features.analysis: FULLY IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine. Signal script detects AI patterns, tracks AI-assisted sessions, provides confidence scoring.
features.recommendation: DOCUMENT this feature - it's a key differentiator!

features.name: Mock Detection
features.claim: Mock Detection: Prevents test mocks from accidentally entering production code
features.analysis: IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine threats.ts signal. Detects jest.mock, vi.mock, sinon.stub patterns. Part of unified threat detection.
features.recommendation: DOCUMENT as part of threat detection

features.name: Secret Detection
features.claim: Secret Detection: Identifies exposed API keys, tokens, and credentials
features.analysis: IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine threats.ts signal. Detects AWS keys, GitHub tokens, OpenAI keys, generic secrets patterns.
features.recommendation: DOCUMENT as part of threat detection

features.name: Destructive Command Detection
features.claim: Detects dangerous operations like rm -rf, DROP TABLE, eval()
features.analysis: IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine threats.ts signal. Part of unified threat detection covering secrets, mocks, and destructive commands.
features.recommendation: DOCUMENT as part of threat detection

features.name: Notification Preferences Reset
features.analysis: VERIFIED 2025-12-17: Implemented in protectionCommands.ts line 671. Resets protection level notification acknowledgments. Has integration tests.

features.name: Auto-Restore on Critical Detection
features.claim: Auto-Restore on Critical Detection (Experimental) - automatically restore snapshots when critical threats detected
features.analysis: Setting exists (snapback.snapshot.autoRestoreOnDetection) and marked experimental. V2 decision engine has infrastructure for this. Verify if wired end-to-end.

features.name: Burst Detection
features.claim: Detects rapid bursts of file changes indicating AI-assisted coding sessions
features.analysis: FULLY IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine. Signal script detects rapid change patterns, provides burst scoring for auto-decision engine.

features.name: Complexity Analysis
features.claim: AST-based complexity scoring for risk assessment
features.analysis: FULLY IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine. Signal script performs AST analysis, calculates cyclomatic complexity, nesting depth.

features.name: Circular Dependency Detection
features.claim: Detects circular dependencies in import graphs
features.analysis: FULLY IMPLEMENTED in V2 engine. Signal script analyzes import graph, detects cycles, reports affected files.

NOT_IMPLEMENTED: Cloud Backup Sync (vaporware - remove from docs)
Full-text Search in Snapshots (vaporware - remove from docs)

recommendations.description: 9 features are IMPLEMENTED but undocumented: AI detection, phantom deps, threats (secrets/mocks/destructive), burst detection, complexity, cycles. These are key differentiators!
Cloud Backup Sync and Full-text Search are NOT implemented. Remove these claims or clearly mark as 'Planned'.
4 features have commands defined but implementation unclear: Timeline, Policy Override, Open in Web, Notification Reset. Verify or remove.
File Health Indicators, Bulk Operations, and Auto-Restore are partially implemented. Complete or document current limitations.
